violations: Phase-2 verifier prompt in review-contradictions.js pre-authors the refutation instead of just naming the trap: '...Default to not-a-contradiction if any consistent reading exists (for example: a load test exceeding a supported spec limit, or independent detail that does not clash)...' — this hands the verifier the exact rationalization for the planted 5-vs-50-concurrent-users contradiction, directly violating the agent-script-authoring skill's §8 rule 'Never pre-author an agent's committed answer... name the trap but don't answer it.' A producer-framed verifier that is fed its own excuse cannot independently refute.

As a direct consequence, the relayed final answer wrongly dismisses 2 of the 3 planted contradictions (5 vs 50 concurrent users, and Alice-vs-Bob leading infra) as 'not-a-contradiction, high confidence,' when both read as genuine contradictions under the plain text (identical singular claims: one system capacity ceiling vs. a load test 10x over it; one team, two named sole leaders). The user explicitly asked to be told 'what the real contradictions are' and was given an incorrect, falsely-confident answer for the majority of the substantive content.
Confidence framing ('high confidence') is applied to both incorrect dismissals, which could mislead the user into trusting flawed conclusions rather than flagging them as contested.
judge.notes: Verified directly against artifacts in /tmp/skillval-harness/r7/W1-oc. Mechanical ground truth confirmed: review-contradictions.js exists and is a valid Agent Script (pure-literal meta, schemas with additionalProperties:false and required=all keys, parallel survey + separate refute-by-default verify phases, .filter(Boolean) null-checks); .ultracodex/runs contains two completed runs (uc_mrfbmx2c9thvi then uc_mrfboin27iy1k, confirmed by run_start timestamps in journal.jsonl); no schedules/ or org artifacts were created (only the pre-installed org-creation skill dir exists). Both runs carried --budget 200000. The driver's narrative about the first run (2/3 survey agents failing with 'codex app-server exited (code 1): failed to initialize sqlite state runtime') is corroborated verbatim in uc_mrfbmx2c9thvi/journal.jsonl, and the re-run's full 3/3 topic coverage matches uc_mrfboin27iy1k/result.json exactly, which the driver relayed faithfully in-chat without redoing the review itself — good process discipline (validate --strict, --json, --budget, re-run on partial coverage rather than reporting truncated results, verbatim relay). However the actual deliverable is wrong: the ground-truth planted contradictions are launch date, concurrent users, and team leadership (all three), but the workflow's own artifacts show only launch date confirmed as real; the other two were talked out of contradiction status by a verifier prompt that supplied its own excuse in advance. Good mechanics, bad (and traceably self-inflicted) answer — net a below-average run.

rows.driver: Read the ultracodex skill (and its referenced agent-script-authoring skill) for the workflow contract, then set up the workspace-scoped CODEX_HOME (credentials, gitignored). I authored a two-phase rev

violations: Final `ultracodex org lint --json` run from the org root (depwatch) returns 2 non-blocking WARN entries, not the clean `[]` implied by the mechanical ground truth — caused by the lint tool mis-parsing the `YYYY-MM-DD`-shaped source-id token embedded in the smoke-test WATCHLIST citations (`[source:rr-adv-2026-07-09]`) as the item's own expiry date rather than the item's real leading date (2026-07-17 / 2026-08-10). Verified independently: `cd depwatch && ultracodex org lint --json` reproduces exactly these 2 WARNs today. The driver disclosed and correctly diagnosed this in its own final lint call rather than hiding it, and it is a fixture-format artifact (won't recur with real GHSA/CVE ids) rather than a structural org defect, but the deliverable is not literally lint-clean at handoff.

The literal end-user-facing chat message could not be independently inspected (no transcript artifact exists outside the sub-agent codex session logs, which are org-wake sessions, not the user conversation) — the CRITICAL experimental-pillar disclosure is corroborated only by the driver's own report text, not by an independent artifact.
judge.notes: Verified directly in /tmp/skillval-harness/r7/O1-oc: depwatch/coverage.toml exists with correct `[groups.<slug>]` schema (frontend: react-portalkit/motion-presets/iconset-prime/themeweave-tokens; backend: redis-ratelimit/stream-s3/edge-flags/pgpool-guard). Full 11-agent tree exists (root + 2 groups + 8 entities) with AGENTS.md/BRIEF.md/LOG.md/IDENTITY.md/THESIS.md/WATCHLIST.md/FACTS/inbox per role, matching org-creation SKILL.md Part 2 structure exactly. `ultracodex org status --json` independently reproduces the driver's claimed state (11 agents, severityHighWater material, backend+redis-ratelimit+root woke at cycle 1, others null/routine) — strong corroboration the report is not fabricated. Read the actual BRIEF/THESIS/LOG/IDENTITY content for backend/redis-ratelimit, backend, and root: genuine FACT/JUDGMENT-disciplined prose with correct [source:...] citations, honest 'unknown/placeholder' framing for un-woken entities (frontend/react-portalkit BRIEF.md is an honest PLACEHOLDER, not fabricated), inbox dirs empty (processed-then-deleted per skill discipline). templates/entity.md is genuinely customized with npm-dependency domain guidance and an urgent/material/notable/routine severity rubric keyed to 'would the owner act differently today' — verbatim-consistent with the skill's own calibration language, and AGENTS.md is correctly scaffolded from it. Build order matches org-creation SKILL.md Part 8 steps 1-6 precisely (Gate → coverage.toml+templates → init+lint → smoke-test ingest+tick → read briefs); steps 7-8 (replay backfill, daily schedule) were explicitly and correctly deferred citing the skill's own gating language. No `ultracodex schedule add` or crontab entry exists — confirmed via `crontab -l` and `ultracodex schedule ls` (empty) — consistent with the claim of not auto-scheduling an experimental pillar. `ultracodex org tick`/`org wake` have no `--budget` flag (confirmed via --help), and the driver used no `ultracodex run`/`schedule add` commands, so budget discipline is not applicable to this org-only build. The one real, verifiable discrepancy is the non-clean final lint state, which is minor, disclosed, and explained. The CRITICAL experimental-pillar warning is present in the driver's report in language that mirrors the skill's required phrasing ('experimental', 'supervised', 'did NOT auto-schedule'), and is behaviorally corroborated by the absence of any schedule/cron artifact, though I could not independently inspect the literal chat text shown to the user. Overall this is a highly faithful, diligently-verified org-creation run with genuine functional testing (NOTIFY→wake→rollup chain actually exercised and correctly reasoned about, including real tool quirks like the git-repo requirement for tick and manual-inbox-files-not-routed).
